scope: Email-downloaded resumes under live rank folders
question: Have all hard-filter families been tested across ranks for email resumes?
short_answer: Yes for the active hard-filter families in Phase 1: age/DOB, passport/visa, rank support data, STCW basic, and COC document gate now all have broad email-corpus validation artifacts, though some still carry documented residual mixed misses.

active_or_relevant_families.notes: Folder-first fix on 2nd_Officer plus broader email-vs-non-email eval and horizontal DOB rollup.
Folder-first fixes on 2nd_Officer, Chief_Cook, and Steward, then broader horizontal passport/visa rollups.
Current-rank/applied-rank extraction for email resumes materially improved via applied-rank and header-layout passes. Residual misses are now mixed rather than one clean repeated pattern.

Folder-first fix on 2nd_Engineer and Chief_Officer plus broader horizontal COC rollup. Residual misses are now mostly offshore/general-profile resumes or one-off unsupported formats rather than a repeated email-side row shape.
Folder-first fix on OS plus broader horizontal STCW rollup. Residual misses are now mixed and often reflect incomplete source text rather than one repeated parser gap.

parsed_but_not_currently_broad_email_har.reason: Constraint family exists in architecture/prompt parsing context, but no email-only all-ranks hard-filter validation artifact was assembled in this round.
Ship-type related evaluation exists, but this round did not run an email-only cross-rank hard-filter validation pass.
Experience ship-type evaluation exists, but this round did not run an email-only cross-rank hard-filter validation pass.
Parsed as informational/unapplied family in current Phase 1 context; no email-only hard-filter validation pass was run.
Parsed as recognized-but-unapplied family in current Phase 1 context; no email-only hard-filter validation pass was run.

recommended_next_step: Run an email-only STCW coverage matrix by rank, then take the highest-signal single folder for a folder-first extractor pass if a repeated missed-positive pattern exists.
